chief_complaint: A 30-year-old male with a history of Maple syrup urine disease and Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus presented with recurrent instances of diabetic ketoacidosis.
history: The patient had a history of Maple syrup urine disease diagnosed ante-natally and managed with strict dietary restrictions. He developed Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus and experienced recurrent episodes of diabetic ketoacidosis, necessitating liver transplant.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed a stable patient with no acute distress.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: glucose 250 mg/dL, electrolytes within normal limits. Imaging: not mentioned. Liver function tests: not mentioned.
